title: Glockenspiel - Tuned Metal-Bar Idiophone Build Packet
honest_uncertainties: Workbook K=204431 is a literature-derived first-pass; per-mill-lot K may differ by 5-10%. Pilot calibration from C5/A5/C7 measurements is the recalibration step.
Grommet durometer (Shore 30A/50A/70A) is TBD until durometer pilot rig is run.
Frame ergonomics (player reach, accidental row offset) is TBD until full-size taped layout is validated.
All supplier prices in sourcing.csv are explicitly marked 'not verified'.
No measured tuning data exists yet; all frequencies in family-spec.csv are target predictions.
Native SolidWorks files are not committed; cad/ folder defines the global-variable and design-table contract.
No process photos are committed yet; photo-shotlist.md documents the build-log image plan.

validator_disposition.expected_finding_1: README.md is suspiciously short and matches the minimal scaffold pattern.
validator_disposition.expected_finding_2: README.md hero image / scaffold pattern matches.
validator_disposition.user_direction: User directed that README.md be left untouched per their 'preserve existing user changes' guidance. Both findings are acknowledged and accepted.

notes: PDF generated via weasyprint 68.1 from print-packet.html with custom @page styling (60 pages).

python-pptx and weasyprint were installed via pip with --break-system-packages on the WSL Python 3.12 runtime to support real binary deliverables. To replicate elsewhere: `pip install --user --break-system-packages python-pptx weasyprint`.
Pandoc emitted cosmetic TeX-math warnings during HTML render where '$' appears in CSV cost columns; the warnings do not affect the output.
wolfram/instrument-model.wl is the v4.2-style packet; wolfram-starter.wl is the 3-line bridge that loads it. Run via `wolframscript -file wolfram-starter.wl` once a local Wolfram is available.
